summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of a legendary night highlighted by a soaring Dark Star and flawless performances from start to finish.
review: The recording is praised as the best‑mix, high‑clarity soundboard tape (with only a tiny early scratch), making every nuance audible. The show itself is described as a signature Dead performance – Dark Star’s epic jam, a beautiful Morning Dew, and a string of tight, inspired renditions from Bird Song to Big River. Reviewers are overwhelmingly enthusiastic, calling it one of the greatest shows ever captured.

ai_rating.rationale: Two glowing 5‑star reviews praise both musical performance and recording quality; a single low‑rating review is vague. Weighted assessment yields a high overall score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Exceptionally clear mix, crisp detail throughout; reviewer notes "best sound" and "best mix" with only a slight scratch at the start of the tape.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: Tight, high‑energy first set that builds into a soaring second set; transitions are seamless and the band stays in an elevated groove throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for leading the Dark Star jam, delivering a beautiful spin on Morning Dew, and sounding visibly pleased.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for strong vocal contributions (Cumberland) and a memorable intro that reminded reviewers of his Spectrum show banter.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir’s vocals described as "nailed" with great harmonies on Jack Straw; overall solid rhythm‑guitar work.
